content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Living Document
Purpose: Index for the Jobs filters collection-style App Behavior corpus example.
Audience: Agents and engineers learning how portable UX items compose into collection files.
Keep In Sync With: UX schemas, model docs, and single-item Jobs filters examples.
Update When: Collection layout, envelope rules, required UX artifact types, or Jobs filters examples change.
Does Not Cover: Runtime implementation, controller ownership, store design, or generated Markdown output.

content.title: Jobs Filters Collection Example
body: This folder shows how the UX system can represent a Jobs filters corpus as collection files while preserving the same portable item schema used by split files and database documents.
The example is intentionally UX-only. It uses Jobs filters as the reference domain, but JSON rows do not treat runtime files, controllers, stores, or code paths as governing UX authority.
Files are grouped in a representative authoring order.
1. [Package Manifest](00-package-manifest.example.json)
2. [Feature And Subfeature Collection](01-feature-and-subfeature.collection.example.json)
3. [Journey And Flow Collection](02-journey-and-flow.collection.example.json)
4. [Requirement Collection](03-requirements.collection.example.json)
5. [Observable Scenario Collection](04-observable-scenarios.collection.example.json)
6. [Acceptance Criteria Collection](05-acceptance-criteria.collection.example.json)
7. [Current Desired Mapping Collection](06-current-desired-mappings.collection.example.json)
8. [Pending Decision Collection](07-pending-decisions.collection.example.json)
9. [Coverage Index](08-coverage-index.example.json)

content.title: Routing
body: Use this example area when the current task needs a concrete reference for a jobs-filters collection App Behavior example pattern.
Does not own target behavior, source authority, implementation details, or proof. Example facts must not be copied into a target corpus unless separately supported by that target authority.
Stop when the agent can state the example pattern being borrowed, the target artifact it informs, and the target source authority that must govern real behavior.
